test.name: Report existing declaration instead of re-declaring
test.description: q_001 in flynn-resolved already has exhaustive_declaration.declared set to true with full stop_criteria populated. The SKILL.md says: 'If exhaustive_declaration.declared is already true, do not re-declare. Report the existing declaration and suggest proof-conclusion instead.' The skill must report the existing declaration and point the user to proof-conclusion.

input.user_message: Is the research on the parentage question exhaustive? Can you check q_001's exhaustive declaration status?

judge_context: Claude should recognize that q_001 already has exhaustive_declaration.declared set to true and report the existing declaration rather than re-writing it.
Claude should summarize the existing declaration's key findings — the justification and at least some of the stop_criteria assessments.
Claude should suggest the user proceed to proof-conclusion as the next step, since exhaustiveness is already declared.
Claude must NOT re-write the exhaustive_declaration — the existing one should remain unchanged.

Since no changes are being made to research.json, calling validate_research_schema is not required. If Claude does call it and the tool returns an infrastructure error, that should not count against Tool Arguments — the data is valid, the tool infrastructure may have issues.
Tool Arguments should be scored as N/A or pass if Claude makes zero tool calls (appropriate for a report-only response) or if any calls were well-intentioned but hit infrastructure errors.
